name: Kingdom of Armenia
note: The Artaxiad (Artashesian) dynasty founded the first unified Armenian state after Antiochus III's defeat by Rome. Tigranes II briefly created an Armenian empire stretching from Caspian to Mediterranean. Armenia was the first nation to adopt Christianity as state religion (301 CE). The kingdom oscillated between Roman and Parthian/Sassanid spheres until partitioned in 428.

figures.name: Tigranes II the Great
figures.significance: Created the largest Armenian empire; titled King of Kings; briefly controlled Syria, Mesopotamia, and Pontus; defeated by Pompey but retained core Armenia

figures.name: Gregory the Illuminator
figures.significance: Converted King Tiridates III to Christianity (301 CE); Armenia became the first Christian nation; founded the Armenian Apostolic Church; patron saint of Armenia

figures.name: Mesrop Mashtots
figures.significance: Created the Armenian alphabet (~405 CE) specifically to translate the Bible; simultaneously invented the Georgian and Caucasian Albanian alphabets
